title: Detect Cycle in an Undirected Graph
coreIdea: Determine if an undirected graph contains a cycle by traversing with BFS or DFS while tracking the parent node, since revisiting a non-parent already-visited node indicates a cycle.
recognitionCues: graph may be disconnected, requiring checks from multiple starting points
approach: For each unvisited node, run BFS/DFS. Track the parent of each visited node. If a neighbor is visited and is not the current node's parent, a cycle is detected. Repeat for all disconnected components.
